concerns.text: Model uses Normal likelihood for cascade size, but cascade size is count data (non-negative integers, typically skewed). Normal likelihood violates construct validity for the outcome. Use Poisson or Negative Binomial likelihood instead.

User-susceptibility proxy (degree ≥ 2, shares ≥ 1) measures activity level, not susceptibility to misinformation. This construct validity gap means the predictor does not measure the named construct. Define a validated susceptibility metric or rename the construct.

Sample size justification marked [deferred]. Hierarchical models require sufficient levels of random effects (users, messages) for stable variance estimates. Specify minimum cascades/users/messages needed for power.
k-fold CV (FR-005) does not specify fold construction for hierarchical data. Standard k-fold may leak information across users/messages. Specify leave-one-user-out or grouped CV to prevent leakage.

Core datasets (PolitiFact, SNAP Twitter cascades) have NO verified sources. Research question depends on cascade topology data that may not exist in verified form. Without verified data, the research question may be unanswerable.
